meaning: vi.干涉,干预,插入,介入,(指时间)介于其间v.干涉
examples: The problem lies in deciding when to intervene.
The security forces had to intervene to prevent the situation worsening.
They remain unwilling to intervene militarily in what could be an unending war.
That's the core of the problem and the reason Buffett was asked to intervene.
Unless other factors intervene, adding more of these gases will tend to warm the atmosphere.
